objective: Add a no-secret Remote MCP operator connectivity preflight that proves fixture request/response and reports current-machine live readiness without exposing secrets.
scope_boundary: Modify the Remote MCP thin client, operator preflight script, focused tests, runbook, governance registries, progress/backlog mirrors, issue #380 planning/validation/closeout artifacts, preflight evidence, and generated graph/wiki artifacts.
Reuse the existing Remote MCP bridge and monitor contracts; do not expose new Remote MCP tools.
Do not commit Cloudflare credentials, bridge tokens, JWTs, HMAC keys, raw PII, production monitor payloads, or raw MCP payloads.
out_of_scope: Installing launchd on the operator machine from this PR.
Configuring production Cloudflare Access or bridge credentials.
Claiming production live request/response readiness when live setup is absent.
research_summary: Issue #380 asks for an operator-safe preflight that answers whether Remote MCP request/response works from this machine now. The existing Stage D runner proves the full bridge acceptance path, while this slice adds a faster one-shot `som.ping` readiness check with fixture coverage and live fail-closed behavior.

sprints.name: Operator connectivity preflight
sprints.goal: Provide a no-secret fixture/live preflight for current-machine Remote MCP `som.ping` request/response readiness.
tasks: Move #378 from active mirrors to Done and mark #380 active.
Add the operator connectivity preflight script with fixture and live modes.
Add `SOM_REMOTE_MCP_JWT` fallback support to the thin client environment loader.
Add focused tests for fixture e2e, live missing-config fail-closed behavior, CLI JSON output, launchctl absence, and JWT fallback.
Update runbook, service registry, feature registry, and data dictionary surfaces.
Generate fixture and current-machine live fail-closed evidence, run local gates, open PR, merge after checks pass, and close #380.
acceptance_criteria: Backlog/progress mirrors track #380 as active before implementation.
Preflight emits JSON with `ready`, `mode`, checks, and recommended action without secret values.
Fixture e2e proves `som.ping` request/response through the thin client.
Live e2e either proves `som.ping` request/response or fails closed before request when required configuration is missing.
Launchd template/install/load status is reported without reading or printing credentials.
Runbook documents how to answer whether Remote MCP messaging works now.
Evidence scan proves preserved artifacts contain no raw SSNs, bearer-token material, Cloudflare Access markers, JWT fragments, credentials, or raw MCP payloads.
Local tests and Local CI Gate pass.
Final AC: GitHub PR checks pass before closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Identify required docs, registry, mirror, artifact, and validation surfaces for issue #380.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended runbook, service registry, feature registry, data dictionary, backlog/progress mirrors, issue artifacts, evidence, and local validation commands.

alternate_model_review.summary: Accepted the #380 governance surface and validation checklist for the no-secret operator connectivity preflight.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #380 may update the Remote MCP thin client, connectivity preflight script, focused tests, runbook, registries, backlog/progress mirrors, preflight evidence, validation, closeout, and generated graph/wiki artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the operator connectivity preflight, validate locally, open PR, merge after checks pass, and close issue #380.

material_deviation_rules: If any preserved artifact includes secrets, JWT fragments, Cloudflare Access material, bearer-token material, raw PII, or raw MCP payloads, remove the artifact and rerun from sanitized inputs.
If production live configuration is absent, preserve live fail-closed evidence only; do not claim live request/response readiness.
If launchd is not installed or loaded, report a warning only for one-shot request/response readiness and keep recurring monitor setup as a separate operational action.
